title: Today's AI Brief: Are AI chatbots making us lose control of our brains?
summary: Exploring the impact of AI chatbots on our brains and Apple's potential plans for AirPods with cameras.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Are AI chatbots making us lose control of our brains?
imageCaption: Are AI chatbots making us lose control of our brains?
imageSourceTitle: Are AI chatbots making us lose control of our brains?

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your daily AI briefing. I've been catching up on some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. It seems like AI chatbots are getting smarter by the minute, but at what cost? I've been attending SXSW London and there's been a lot of buzz around AI. I had a chance to sit down with Gloria Mark, a psychologist at the University of California, Irvine, who's been studying the effects of AI on our brains.

Gloria's work suggests that our increasing reliance on AI-powered tools might be changing the way we think and interact with the world. For instance, have you ever found yourself mindlessly scrolling through social media or relying on AI assistants to get through your day? It's not hard to see how this could impact our focus and productivity. I'm not sure if I'm ready to blame AI chatbots for my lack of focus, but it's definitely food for thought.

In other news, Wired reports that Apple might be planning to add cameras to its next-generation AirPods. Now, I'm not sure what kind of functionality cameras in earbuds would unlock, but I'm guessing it wouldn't be just for taking selfies. The idea does raise some interesting questions about data boundaries and safety, though. I mean, can you imagine walking down the street with earbuds that have cameras? It's a bit unsettling.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
